markdown.markdown:
This project is a little different, as it is not only about data, but also about the people who work with it. It started from a simple observation: at the IRD center in Montpellier, many people were working with data… without really knowing each other. We felt there was clearly something valuable to build from that.

Together with a colleague from another research unit, we launched in 2022 a community group focused on biostatistics: the Biostat group. The idea was to create a space for exchange, sharing practices, and simply connecting people.

Over time, the group grew, discussions became richer, and we eventually merged with another collective more focused on bioinformatics. This led to the creation of RADO, which I co-led until my departure.

Helping run this network was a truly valuable experience for me. It allowed me to develop new skills, but above all to enjoy many great moments of sharing, collaboration, and friendliness.

And the nicest part is that the network is still active and continues to grow thanks to the energy of the people leading it today.

Take a look here: [RADO](https://rado.pages.ird.fr/website/)

-------
## Groupe d'animation autour de la data et des biostat

Ce projet est un peu à part, car il ne parle pas seulement de données, mais surtout des personnes qui travaillent avec. Il est né d’un constat simple : au centre IRD de Montpellier, beaucoup de gens manipulaient des données… sans vraiment se connaître. Et on s’est dit qu’il y avait clairement quelque chose à construire là-dessus.

Avec un collègue d’une autre unité, on a donc lancé en 2022 un groupe d’animation autour des biostatistiques : le groupe Biostat. L’idée était de créer un espace d’échange, de partage de pratiques, et tout simplement de mettre en lien les personnes.

Au fil du temps, le groupe a grandi, les échanges se sont enrichis, et on a fini par fusionner avec un autre collectif plus orienté bioinformatique. De là est né le RADO, que j’ai co-animé jusqu’à mon départ.

Animer ce réseau a été une expérience vraiment précieuse pour moi. Ça m’a permis de développer de nouvelles compétences, mais surtout de vivre de très bons moments de partage, de collaboration et de convivialité.

Et le plus chouette, c’est que le réseau continue de vivre et de s’agrandir grâce à l’énergie des personnes qui l’animent aujourd’hui.

Jetez-y un œil ici : [RADO](https://rado.pages.ird.fr/website/)

## Data & Biostatistics Community Initiative

This project is a little different, as it is not only about data, but also about the people who work with it. It started from a simple observation: at the IRD center in Montpellier, many people were working with data… without really knowing each other. We felt there was clearly something valuable to build from that.

Together with a colleague from another research unit, we launched in 2022 a community group focused on biostatistics: the Biostat group. The idea was to create a space for exchange, sharing practices, and simply connecting people.

Over time, the group grew, discussions became richer, and we eventually merged with another collective more focused on bioinformatics. This led to the creation of RADO, which I co-led until my departure.

Helping run this network was a truly valuable experience for me. It allowed me to develop new skills, but above all to enjoy many great moments of sharing, collaboration, and friendliness.

And the nicest part is that the network is still active and continues to grow thanks to the energy of the people leading it today.

Take a look here: [RADO](https://rado.pages.ird.fr/website/)

-------
